question: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of May? [Polymarket]
newsShortReason: Limited resumption of Strait of Hormuz traffic amid ongoing U.S.-Iran negotiations.
newsKeyEvidence: As of May 25, 2026, the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed to commercial shipping, with only 2 vessel transits recorded on May 17, compared to a typical 95 per day. ([straits.live](https://straits.live/?utm_source=openai)) | On May 23, 2026, President Trump announced that a deal to end the war with Iran would be finalized 'shortly,' aiming to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. ([axios.com](https://www.axios.com/2026/05/23/us-iran-trump-deal-war?utm_source=openai)) | Despite the announcement, as of May 24, 2026, oil prices dropped by approximately $5 per barrel, indicating cautious optimism but highlighting the need for a signed agreement to restore shipping confidence and reopen the strait safely. ([axios.com](https://www.axios.com/2026/05/24/oil-prices-iran-war-hormuz-strait-trump-tehran-peace-talks?utm_source=openai))
